composite.summary: Quant composite implies roughly HOLD; my read agrees — Maersk sits at sub-book value (P/B 0.71) with a golden-cross uptrend and an elevated-but-fading freight tailwind (GSCPI 1.25). The strategic deep-value/SOP case (Logistics priced near zero) tilts me modestly buy-side, but decaying spot rates and 2026 orderbook oversupply cap conviction, leaving a mild HOLD with a slight accumulation lean.

tactical.bear_case: Spot freight rates (Drewry WCI) have been rolling over from Red Sea-driven highs as capacity normalizes; if a Red Sea/Suez de-escalation headline hits, container rates and AMKBY sentiment gap down quickly given the stock's beta to spot rates.
tactical.bull_case: GSCPI at 1.25 (elevated) plus any renewed Red Sea disruption or port congestion re-spikes WCI, and the golden-cross uptrend continues to grind higher — RSI 56 leaves room before overbought.
tactical.falsifier: A daily close below the 50-day SMA ($12.30) on rising volume would flip the tactical read from mildly-trim to buy-the-dip.

positional.bear_case: Drewry WCI composite has fallen materially from its 2024 Red Sea peak; as 2M/Gemini alliance capacity and new-build deliveries hit the water into 2026, the rate normalization compresses the freight tailwind the engine is crediting, and the positional tailwind reverses.
positional.bull_case: Persistent Red Sea diversions keep effective capacity tight and ton-miles elevated; combined with restocking demand, WCI holds above breakeven and Maersk's ocean margins surprise to the upside on the next print, extending the golden-cross.
positional.falsifier: GSCPI dropping below 0.5 or WCI breaking below full-cycle breakeven (~$1,500/FEU) would flip the freight tailwind to a headwind and turn this layer negative-to-trim... i.e., sign flips positive/trim decisively.

strategic.bear_case: Container shipping enters structural oversupply in 2026-27 as the orderbook (~25%+ of fleet) delivers into softening demand; if Red Sea reopens, effective capacity surges, freight rates collapse toward cash-cost, and even a 0.71 P/B can compress further as book value erodes with newbuild write-downs and buyback-funded cash depletes.
strategic.bull_case: Maersk's fortress balance sheet (net cash for much of the cycle), aggressive buyback, and the Gemini network reliability gains let it defend margins; the Logistics segment re-rates toward integrator peers, unlocking sum-of-parts value the market currently ignores — 0.71 P/B on a franchise trading below replacement is deep value.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained collapse in global trade volumes (CPB World Trade Monitor turning negative YoY) combined with the orderbook flooding capacity would flip strategic to trim/positive despite the P/B discount, as trough earnings deepen into losses.

A -11% single-day gap on an earnings day for a 1.7-beta name is not automatically a buy — the market is repricing something real. If EPS revisions turn negative post-print, positional mean-reversion is fighting a genuine estimate cut and the stock grinds lower toward the 52w low and below — buying a broken chart mid-repricing is exactly the setup that burned this layer yesterday.
